varieties.name: Black Beauty
varieties.traits: Dark green, almost black skin; cylindrical shape; 6-8 inches at harvest
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent for Brisbane's subtropical climate. Bush-type plant, heat tolerant, handles humidity well. Most popular green zucchini. Matures 50-63 days.
varieties.availability: Widely available in Queensland nurseries and seed suppliers

varieties.name: Costata Romanesco
varieties.traits: Dark green with light green striping; prominent ribs; 12-18 inches
varieties.flesh_fruit: Dense, nutty flavor; tender even when large
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate; produces large male blossoms for cooking
varieties.growing_notes: Superior flavor, suited to Brisbane climate. Handles heat well, traditional Italian heirloom. Best picked at 4-6 inches. Some parthenocarpic ability. Matures 52-60 days.
varieties.availability: Specialty seed companies, heirloom suppliers in Australia

varieties.name: Cocozelle
varieties.traits: Dark green with light green stripes; long cylindrical; slight ribbing

varieties.name: Grey Zucchini
varieties.traits: Gray mottled skin; cylindrical shape; 6-8 inches harvest
varieties.growing_notes: Popular in subtropical regions. Heat tolerant, handles Brisbane humidity. Semi-spiny bush type. Matures 55 days.

varieties.name: Round Zucchini (Ronde de Nice)
varieties.traits: Round shape, 2-4 inches diameter; mottled green coloring
varieties.yield_notes: Very productive; flower to fruit in one week
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent for Brisbane, perfect for stuffing. Compact plant, ideal for containers. Harvest at 2-3 inch diameter. Matures 45-50 days.

varieties.name: Early Summer Crookneck
varieties.traits: Curved neck, yellow bumpy skin; 6 inches at harvest
varieties.flesh_fruit: Creamy white, buttery flavor, meaty texture
varieties.growing_notes: One of oldest squash types, pre-European contact. Excellent for Brisbane subtropical climate. Heat tolerant, handles humidity. Considered best-tasting yellow crookneck. Matures 50-60 days.
varieties.availability: Common in Queensland nurseries and seed racks

varieties.name: Early Prolific Straightneck
varieties.traits: Straight neck, creamy yellow, smooth skin; 6-8 inches

varieties.name: Golden Summer Crookneck
varieties.traits: Golden yellow, curved neck, warted skin when mature

varieties.name: Early White Bush Scallop
varieties.origin: Pre-1700s, one of oldest squash varieties
varieties.traits: Pale green to white, scalloped edges, 4-6 inches

varieties.name: Benning's Green Tint
varieties.origin: Washington DC (Benning), 1914, developed by Charles N. Farr
varieties.traits: Pale green to white, scalloped, 2-3 inches ideal
varieties.growing_notes: Rated best-tasting summer squash by many. Performs excellently in Brisbane's subtropical climate. Compact productive plants. Matures 50-55 days.

varieties.name: Yellow Bush Scallop
varieties.traits: Golden yellow, scalloped edges, 3-4 inches
varieties.growing_notes: Colorful addition to Brisbane gardens. Bush habit, heat tolerant. Decorative and delicious. Matures 50-54 days.

varieties.name: Sunburst
varieties.traits: Bright yellow, scalloped, 2-3 inches ideal
varieties.growing_notes: Powdery mildew resistant - excellent for Brisbane humidity. Award-winning hybrid. Early and productive. Matures 50-52 days.

varieties.name: Waltham Butternut
varieties.origin: Waltham, Massachusetts, 1960s by Robert Young; AAS Winner 1970
varieties.traits: Tan skin, bell-shaped, straight cylindrical neck; 9 inches long, 4-6 lbs
varieties.flesh_fruit: Deep orange, dry, sweet nutty flavor, smooth texture
varieties.growing_notes: Most popular butternut, suitable for Brisbane if started early. Requires warm season. Resistant to squash vine borers. Excellent keeper (5+ months). Best flavor after 2 months storage. Matures 85-110 days.

varieties.name: Table Queen Acorn
varieties.origin: Iowa Seed Company, 1913; descended from Arikara Indian squash 1800s; AAS Winner 1939
varieties.traits: Dark green, acorn-shaped, deeply ribbed, 4-5.5 inches, 1.5 lbs
varieties.flesh_fruit: Golden yellow (turns orange in storage), sweet, fine-textured, dry and thick
varieties.growing_notes: Sets standard for acorn squash. Suitable for Brisbane with early start. Excellent for baking. Good storage capability. Matures 75-90 days.

varieties.name: Bottle Gourd (Lauki, Doodhi, Sorakkai) 🏆
varieties.origin: Ancient cultivation in India, traditional throughout Kerala
varieties.traits: Light green, elongated or bulbous shape; various sizes (bottle-shaped or long cylindrical)
varieties.flesh_fruit: White, tender, mild flavor, juicy texture
varieties.growing_notes: Perfectly adapted to Kerala's monsoon climate. Thrives in high humidity and heat. Grows during summer and monsoon seasons. Essential vegetable in Kerala cuisine. Cooling properties valued in Ayurveda. Matures 60-65 days.
varieties.availability: Universally available in Kerala - local nurseries, seed shops, agricultural markets

varieties.name: Ridge Gourd (Torai, Peerkangai, Jhinga) 🏆
varieties.origin: Traditional throughout India, especially South India and Kerala
varieties.traits: Long cylindrical with prominent vertical ridges; dark green skin; 12-18 inches
varieties.flesh_fruit: Spongy white flesh, mild flavor, tender when young
varieties.growing_notes: Thrives in Kerala's monsoon conditions. Loves warm, sunny, humid environments. Essential in Kerala cooking - curries and stir-fries. Blood purifier, rich in vitamins C and A. Excellent fiber source. Grows year-round in tropical climate.
varieties.availability: Widely available throughout Kerala - every local market and nursery

varieties.name: Ash Gourd (Petha, Kumbalanga, Neer Poosanikai) 🏆
varieties.origin: Native to Southern Asia, traditional in Kerala and South India
varieties.traits: Large, round or oblong; pale green skin with white waxy coating; 10-40 lbs
varieties.flesh_fruit: White, mildly sweet like cucumber, crunchy texture
varieties.yield_notes: Large fruits, moderate yields on vines
varieties.growing_notes: Perfect for Kerala's tropical climate. Warm-season crop loving heat. Highly valued in Ayurvedic medicine for cooling properties. Combats acidity, ulcers, keeps gut healthy. Used in curries, pickles, candies. Excellent storage (months). Grown throughout South India.
varieties.availability: Common throughout Kerala markets and agricultural suppliers

varieties.name: Snake Gourd (Chichinda, Padwal) 🏆
varieties.origin: Native to South India, traditional in Kerala cuisine
varieties.traits: Very long (up to 150cm), slender, twisted like snake; light green with white stripes
varieties.flesh_fruit: Mild, slightly sweet, soft texture when cooked
varieties.growing_notes: Thrives in Kerala's hot and humid tropical climate. Requires trellis support. Popular in Kerala sambar and stir-fries. Fringed white flowers open at night. Harvest immature fruits before fibrous. Strengthens respiratory system, aids weight loss. Available during monsoon season.
varieties.availability: Common in Kerala - local markets and agricultural stores

varieties.name: Bitter Gourd (Karela, Pavakka) 🏆
varieties.origin: Native to Kerala and tropical regions of India
varieties.traits: Bumpy, spiky exterior; oblong shape; dark to light green
varieties.growing_notes: Staple in Kerala kitchen gardens. Thrives in tropical climate. Excellent for managing blood sugar levels. Rich in iron, beta-carotene, vitamins. Valued in Ayurveda for blood purification. Anti-inflammatory properties. Grows throughout summer season in Kerala.
varieties.availability: Universally available - every Kerala market and nursery

varieties.name: Apple Gourd / Tinda (Indian Squash) 🌱
varieties.origin: Native to India, popular in South Asia
varieties.traits: Small, round or oval; green color; slight thorny surface; apple-sized
varieties.flesh_fruit: Mild flavor, takes on spice flavors
varieties.growing_notes: Well-adapted to tropical monsoon climate. Loves humid and hot conditions. Vines grow 1-2 meters. Can be grown in containers with vertical support. Matures 60-70 days. Popular summer vegetable in Kerala.
varieties.availability: Available in Kerala markets during summer season

varieties.name: Yellow Crookneck Squash
varieties.traits: Yellow, curved neck, warted when mature
varieties.growing_notes: Can adapt to Kerala's tropical climate with good drainage. Needs protection from heavy monsoon rains. Best planted after main monsoon period. Heat tolerant. Harvest when 6 inches. Matures 50-60 days.
varieties.availability: Specialty seed suppliers in India, international seed companies

varieties.name: Black Beauty Zucchini
varieties.traits: Dark green, cylindrical, 6-8 inches
varieties.growing_notes: Can be grown in Kerala with proper management. Needs well-drained soil to prevent fungal issues in high humidity. Best grown in drier months (December-March). Requires regular harvest. Popular for international cooking. Matures 50-55 days.
varieties.availability: Select garden centers in Kerala, online seed suppliers
